concept_candidates.label: continent
concept_candidates.description: A continent is a large landmass identified by convention, typically encompassing a single terrestrial region or a landmass plus nearby islands.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a stable, reusable geographic concept that aligns with existing ontology classes and is not a document-specific mention.

concept_candidates.label: TimeInterval
concept_candidates.description: A specific span of time defined by a start and end point, such as a month, year, or other calendar-based period.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a reusable, semantically stable concept that extends the existing TIME class to capture interval-based temporal entities.

concept_candidates.label: City
concept_candidates.description: A human settlement of substantial size, defined as a densely populated place with administrative boundaries and primarily non‑agricultural activities.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a reusable geographic concept that is semantically stable and general enough for ontology modeling.

concept_candidates.label: Social Data Analytics
concept_candidates.description: A research area focused on collecting, processing, and analyzing data from social contexts such as social media, online communities, and other digital social interactions to uncover patterns, insights, and trends.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a stable, reusable research area concept that is general enough to be applied across multiple documents and aligns with the ontology's treatment of research domains.

concept_candidates.label: Natural Resources Solutions
concept_candidates.description: A research area focusing on solutions related to natural resources.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a stable, reusable research area concept that can be applied across multiple documents, fitting ontology-oriented modeling better than treating it as a document-specific mention.

concept_candidates.label: FossilFuel
concept_candidates.description: A non‑renewable hydrocarbon resource formed from the buried remains of prehistoric organisms, extracted from geological reservoirs and used as an energy source and in petrochemical manufacturing.
concept_candidates.justification: Fossil fuel is a stable, general concept that can be reused across documents and fits an ontology of energy resources better than a document‑specific mention.

concept_candidates.label: SocialMediaPlatform
concept_candidates.description: A software platform that enables users to create, share, and aggregate content, facilitating social networking and microblogging.
concept_candidates.justification: Social media platform is a reusable class that represents a type of computing platform used for social networking and microblogging, fitting ontology-oriented modeling rather than a document-specific mention.

ontology_relation_candidates.label: hasNaturalResourceManagement
ontology_relation_candidates.description: Relates an administrative area or organization to its natural resource management program or policy.
ontology_relation_candidates.justification: This relation captures a stable, reusable concept that links regions or organizations to their natural resource management initiatives, which is frequently mentioned across documents and aligns with existing ontology practices.
